azul.title: Azul - Rules
azul.objective: Score the most points by placing tiles on your palace wall. Create rows, columns, and color sets to earn bonus points.
azul.setup: Each round, tiles are randomly drawn and placed on factory displays. The center of the table starts empty.
azul.turn: Pick all tiles of one color from a factory or the center. Place them on one of your 5 pattern lines (rows 1-5). Excess tiles go to your floor line (penalties). If you take from a factory, remaining tiles go to the center.
azul.scoring: When a pattern line is full, one tile moves to the matching wall spot. Score 1 point per tile, plus bonuses for adjacent tiles in the same row/column. Floor line tiles subtract points.
azul.endGame: The game ends when a player completes a horizontal row on their wall. Bonus points: +2 per complete row, +7 per complete column, +10 for all 5 tiles of one color.

petitsChevaux.title: Horse Race - Rules
petitsChevaux.objective: Be the first to move all 4 of your horses from the stable to the finish area.
petitsChevaux.setup: Each player has 4 horses in their stable. Roll a 6 to release a horse onto the board.
petitsChevaux.turn: Roll the dice and move one of your horses forward by the number shown. Rolling a 6 gives you an extra turn and lets you release a horse from the stable.
petitsChevaux.capture: If your horse lands on a space occupied by an opponent's horse, the opponent's horse is sent back to the stable.
petitsChevaux.endGame: Move your horses around the board and into your finish area. The first player to get all 4 horses home wins!

burkutBori.title: Eagles & Wolves - Rules
burkutBori.objective: Be the first player to reach cell 100 on the board.
burkutBori.turn: Roll the dice and advance your piece by the number shown. Players start off the board and enter on their first roll.
burkutBori.eagles: Land on a cell with a golden eagle and soar ahead! Eagles carry you to a higher cell on the board.
burkutBori.wolves: Land on a cell with a grey wolf and slide back. Wolves drag you down to a lower cell.
burkutBori.endGame: The first player to reach exactly cell 100 wins. If your roll would take you past 100, you don't move.

memory.title: Memory - Rules
memory.objective: Find the most matching pairs of cards. Each pair features a Central Asian symbol.
memory.setup: Cards are shuffled and placed face down on the board. The grid size can be customized by the host.
memory.turn: Flip two cards. If they match, you keep the pair and play again. If they don't match, both cards are flipped back and it's the next player's turn.
memory.endGame: The game ends when all pairs are found. The player with the most pairs wins!

backgammon.title: Backgammon - Rules
backgammon.objective: Be the first to bear off all 15 of your checkers from the board.
backgammon.setup: Each player starts with 15 checkers placed on specific points. Gold moves from point 24 toward point 1, Lapis moves from point 1 toward point 24.
backgammon.turn: Roll two dice and move your checkers forward by the values shown. You may split the dice between two checkers or use both on one. Doubles give you four moves instead of two.
backgammon.hitting: If you land on a point with exactly one opponent checker (a blot), it is sent to the bar. That player must re-enter from the bar before making other moves.
backgammon.bearingOff: Once all your checkers are in your home board (last 6 points), you can start bearing them off. Roll a number matching a point with your checker to remove it.
backgammon.endGame: The first player to bear off all 15 checkers wins the game.

toguzKorgool.title: Toguz Korgool - Rules
toguzKorgool.objective: Capture more than 81 stones (out of 162 total). Each player starts with 9 pits containing 9 stones each.
toguzKorgool.setup: The board has two rows of 9 pits. Each pit starts with 9 stones. Each player also has a kazan (scoring pit) that starts empty.
toguzKorgool.turn: Pick up all stones from one of your pits and sow them one by one counter-clockwise. If the last stone lands in an opponent's pit making it even, capture all stones from that pit into your kazan.
toguzKorgool.tuz: If the last stone lands in an opponent's pit making it exactly 3, you may claim that pit as your tuz. All stones landing in a tuz go directly to your kazan. Each player can have at most one tuz, and the 9th pit cannot be a tuz.
toguzKorgool.endGame: The game ends when a player captures more than 81 stones, or when a player cannot move. If both players have exactly 81 stones, it's a draw.
